tool.name: MMseqs2 Sensitive Clustering
tool.command:
mmseqs easy-cluster '$input_fasta' 'result' 'tmp'
@CMD_DBTYPE@
## Pre-filter options @CMD_PREFILTER@ --spaced-kmer-mode $prefilter.spaced_kmer_mode @CMD_SEARCH_COMMON@
## Align options @CMD_ALIGN@ --alignment-mode $align.alignment_mode -e $align.evalue --min-seq-id $min_seq_id -c $cov --cov-mode $cov_mode --max-rejected $align.max_rejected --max-accept $align.max_accept
## Clustering options @CMD_CLUSTERING@
## Misc options @CMD_MISC@
## Common options @CMD_THREADS@ --max-seq-len $common.max_seq_len
## Expert options @CMD_EXPERT@

param.label: Maximum rejected alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped
Maximum accepted alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped

tool.help:
@HELP_HEADER@
**About this tool**
``easy-cluster`` groups sequences into clusters based on sequence similarity using a **cascaded clustering** algorithm. It is the more sensitive of the two MMseqs2 clustering tools. Use **easy-linclust** instead when working with very large datasets (millions of sequences) and linear runtime is needed.
**How it works**
Cascaded clustering runs in three progressive steps:
1. Fast low-sensitivity clustering to group the most similar sequences 2. Re-clustering of representatives at moderate sensitivity 3. Final clustering of remaining representatives at full sensitivity
Results are merged across all steps to produce the final clusters. This multi-pass approach captures both close and distant homologs while remaining faster than all-vs-all comparison.
**easy-cluster vs easy-linclust**
- **easy-cluster**: Cascaded approach, higher sensitivity, super-linear runtime. Best for datasets up to a few million sequences or when maximum sensitivity at the chosen threshold matters. - **easy-linclust**: Linear-time algorithm, slightly less sensitive, can handle hundreds of millions of sequences. Can cluster down to approximately 50% sequence identity.
**Key parameters**
- **Sequence identity (--min-seq-id)**: Minimum identity threshold (0.0-1.0) for cluster membership. - **Coverage (-c, --cov-mode)**: Minimum alignment coverage. Mode 0 uses bidirectional coverage, mode 1 uses target coverage, mode 2 uses query coverage. - **Cluster mode (--cluster-mode)**: Algorithm for resolving clusters from pairwise similarities. Mode 0 (greedy set cover, default) maximizes cluster sizes. Mode 1 (connected component) uses transitive connections. Mode 2 (greedy incremental) is CD-HIT-like. - **Sensitivity (-s)**: Controls prefilter stringency. Auto-adjusted based on ``--min-seq-id`` if not set.
**Outputs**
- **Representative sequences**: One FASTA entry per cluster (the centroid) - **All sequences per cluster**: All sequences grouped by cluster membership - **Cluster TSV**: Tab-separated adjacency list (representative, member) for downstream processing
@HELP_FOOTER@
